Requirement: The vendor must deliver a modern cloud-native IGA platform that automates identity lifecycle management and strengthens access governance across the enterprise.
Acceptance Criteria: Demonstrate a SaaS IGA solution with automated provisioning and deprovisioning, access risk controls, and measurable compliance outcomes. Provide customer references and documented architecture.

Requirement: The IGA solution must support identity lifecycle management for all identity types including employees, contractors, third-party users, non-human identities (NHI), and AI agents.
Acceptance Criteria: Provide end-to-end Joiner-Mover-Leaver automation for all identity types. Include production examples of NHI and AI agent identity management.

Requirement: The platform must scale to support a minimum of 150,000 identities across multiple legal entities without performance degradation.
Acceptance Criteria: Provide performance benchmarks and case studies at scale. Include SLA commitments for provisioning and deprovisioning response times.

Requirement: The vendor must support data residency requirements for Canadian-based identity data in compliance with PIPEDA and applicable provincial privacy legislation.
Acceptance Criteria: Confirm availability of a Canadian data residency option. Provide documentation of data handling, storage locations, and applicable compliance certifications.

Requirement: The solution must automate the full Joiner-Mover-Leaver process driven by authoritative HR system data with no manual intervention required for standard lifecycle events.
Acceptance Criteria: Demonstrate automated provisioning from HR system trigger to application access grant within a defined SLA. Show sample workflows for new hire, role change, and termination scenarios.

Requirement: The IGA platform must support multiple authoritative HR sources simultaneously including Workday and SAP SuccessFactors.
Acceptance Criteria: Provide a list of certified HR system integrations. Demonstrate simultaneous ingestion from two or more HR sources with conflict resolution logic.

Requirement: The solution must manage unique identity identifiers across source systems and integrated applications and support generation of enterprise-wide unique IDs.
Acceptance Criteria: Describe the identity correlation and matching engine. Explain how unique identifiers are generated, managed, and reconciled across disparate systems.

Requirement: The platform must support provisioning to both connected applications via SCIM/API and disconnected applications via flat file or manual task fulfilment.
Acceptance Criteria: Show workflow examples for both connected and disconnected application types. Describe the manual task fulfilment process and SLA.

Requirement: The solution must provide automated access certification campaigns with configurable scope, frequency, and reviewer assignment including manager, application owner, and peer review.
Acceptance Criteria: Demonstrate a certification campaign configuration. Show AI-assisted reviewer recommendations and how they improve decision accuracy.

Requirement: The IGA platform must support risk-based access reviews where high-risk entitlements are prioritized with contextual risk scoring for reviewers.
Acceptance Criteria: Show how risk scores are calculated and surfaced during reviews. Include indicators such as privileged access, SoD conflicts, and dormant accounts.

Requirement: The solution must provide self-service access request capabilities with multi-level approval workflows and integration with ServiceNow for ticket creation and fulfilment.
Acceptance Criteria: Demonstrate the access request portal and a sample approval workflow with ServiceNow integration including bi-directional status sync.

Requirement: The platform must support both role-based (RBAC) and policy-based (PBAC) access control models with the ability to manage and enforce both independently or in combination.
Acceptance Criteria: Provide documentation on RBAC and PBAC model support. Show examples where both are applied simultaneously to a single user population.

Requirement: The IGA solution must include built-in SoD conflict detection across all connected applications with a configurable rule library.
Acceptance Criteria: Demonstrate SoD rule configuration and real-time conflict detection during access requests before provisioning occurs.

Requirement: The platform must support import of existing SoD rulesets and allow ongoing expansion and refinement of rules as business requirements evolve.
Acceptance Criteria: Explain the process for importing existing SoD rules. Describe versioning and update management for the SoD rule library.

Requirement: The solution must provide SoD violation dashboards and reports for compliance and audit teams with drill-down to individual user-entitlement conflicts.
Acceptance Criteria: Show a sample SoD compliance dashboard. Demonstrate drill-down from summary to individual violation detail including remediation status.

Requirement: The IGA platform must support a full enterprise role model including business roles and IT roles with complete lifecycle management from creation to deprecation.
Acceptance Criteria: Describe the role model architecture and lifecycle workflow. Show how business roles are composed from IT roles.

Requirement: The solution must include role mining and role health analytics to identify redundant, unused, or risk-prone roles within the environment.
Acceptance Criteria: Demonstrate role mining using historical access data. Define role health metrics and show how unhealthy roles are identified and remediated.

Requirement: The platform must support AI-generated entitlement descriptions to improve clarity and reduce reviewer fatigue during access certification campaigns.
Acceptance Criteria: Show an example of AI-generated entitlement descriptions. Describe accuracy validation before descriptions are published to reviewers.

Requirement: The IGA solution must provide certified integration with Microsoft Active Directory and Azure Active Directory as both authoritative sources and provisioning targets.
Acceptance Criteria: Confirm AD and Azure AD integration. Provide documentation on sync frequency, attribute mapping, writeback capabilities, and conflict resolution.

Requirement: The platform must support SCIM 2.0 provisioning to all compatible applications and provide an integration framework for custom and legacy systems.
Acceptance Criteria: List all SCIM 2.0 certified integrations. Describe the approach for building connectors for legacy or proprietary applications.

Requirement: The solution must integrate with existing ITSM tooling for access request fulfilment and provisioning events that require manual handling.
Acceptance Criteria: Provide documentation on ITSM integration capabilities. Show ticket creation from a provisioning event and describe bi-directional status sync.

Requirement: The IGA platform must support governance of privileged accounts and service accounts with vaulting and just-in-time access provisioning capabilities.
Acceptance Criteria: Describe native PAM capabilities or certified third-party PAM integrations. Explain how privileged accounts are discovered and governed within the IGA platform.

Requirement: The vendor must hold current SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certifications. Canadian federal compliance (CCCS Medium or equivalent) is preferred.
Acceptance Criteria: Provide current SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certificates. Describe the audit schedule and any additional compliance frameworks supported.

Requirement: The IGA platform must maintain immutable audit logs for all identity events with a minimum 7-year retention period and integration with external SIEM platforms.
Acceptance Criteria: Demonstrate audit log architecture and tamper-protection controls. Provide retention policy documentation and SIEM integration options.

Requirement: The solution must enforce multi-factor authentication for all administrative access to the IGA platform and apply least-privilege principles to platform administrators.
Acceptance Criteria: Describe MFA options for admin access. Show how administrative roles are defined and how least-privilege is enforced within the platform.

Requirement: The IGA solution must leverage AI and machine learning to provide intelligent access recommendations during certifications, supporting auto-approval for low-risk and enhanced review for high-risk items.
Acceptance Criteria: Describe the AI model used for access recommendations. Provide accuracy metrics and false-positive rates from production deployments.

Requirement: The platform must support identity lifecycle management for AI agents and non-human identities with governance policies distinct from human identity management.
Acceptance Criteria: Describe how AI agent identities are onboarded, managed, and decommissioned. Explain how NHI entitlements are reviewed and governed.

Requirement: The solution must provide automated anomaly detection for unusual access patterns with alerting to security operations teams.
Acceptance Criteria: Show anomaly detection scenario examples. Describe the alerting mechanism and SIEM or SOC platform integration options.

Requirement: The IGA platform must be delivered as a multi-tenant SaaS solution with a minimum 99.9% uptime SLA and documented disaster recovery procedures.
Acceptance Criteria: Provide current SLA documentation and historical uptime metrics for the past 12 months. Describe RTO and RPO targets for disaster recovery.

Requirement: The solution must provide a fully documented RESTful API for all platform functions to support customer-built integrations and automation pipelines.
Acceptance Criteria: Provide API documentation and versioning strategy. Share example customer-built integration use cases and SDK availability.

Requirement: The vendor must provide a no-code workflow automation capability allowing non-technical administrators to build and modify identity processes without developer involvement.
Acceptance Criteria: Demonstrate a workflow built using the no-code tool. Describe available triggers, conditions, and actions for non-technical administrators.

Requirement: The vendor must provide a dedicated implementation team with certified IGA consultants and a documented project methodology for enterprise-scale deployments.
Acceptance Criteria: Describe the implementation methodology and team structure. Provide a sample project plan and references from comparable deployments.

Requirement: The solution must include a named customer success program with quarterly business reviews and access to a technical account manager following go-live.
Acceptance Criteria: Describe the post-implementation support model and SLA tiers for P1, P2, and P3 incidents. Include escalation procedures.

Requirement: The vendor must offer administrator certification training and self-service documentation to support ongoing platform education for customer teams.
Acceptance Criteria: List available training programs and certifications. Describe delivery formats and whether training is included in the base license.
